transcript: This is what a typical courtroom looks like. Both criminal and civil cases are heard here every day. Most people know that judges preside over a courtroom and make the decisions, but there are others who play a role in court hearings, such as the clerk who assists the judge. In a criminal trial, the accused will be present, together with his or her defense lawyer who will defend him or her against the charges. The accused will be guarded by the bailiff. The prosecutor will present the case against the accused. The witnesses, including the victim, will be questioned by both the defense and the prosecution. Reporters and members of the public may be present in the viewing gallery. In a civil case, the complainant and defendant will be present, along with their lawyers and witnesses.
